title: calamintha montana subsp. menthifolia
intro: Calamintha montana subsp. menthifolia, commonly known as Mountain Calamint, is a subspecies of the Calamintha montana plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues and relieving pain. The health benefits of Mountain Calamint include its ability to soothe stomach upset, reduce inflammation, and ease menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for women's health issues. Mountain Calamint can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or applied topically as an oil or salve to reap its medicinal benefits.
